slides.title: debug-client Affiliate Program Quarterly Business Review
slides.headline: General performance review remains the primary QBR focus
slides.summary: A comprehensive year-over-year analysis of the debug-client affiliate program's performance, publisher dynamics, and strategic priorities to drive growth and optimise outcomes.

slides.title: Reporting Period
slides.subtitle: Current and YoY comparison windows
slides.callout: All figures are reported in GBP unless otherwise stated. YoY variance is calculated as current period vs comparison period.

slides.title: Program Performance: Executive Summary
slides.summary: The debug-client Affiliate Program delivered mixed results in Jan 2026 – Mar 2026. While AOV grew N/A to - and conversion rate improved N/A, total sales declined N/A YoY driven by a N/A reduction in click volume. Total order value changed N/A to -. Full KPI breakdown follows on the next slides.

slides.title: KPI Summary Table: Volume, Conversion, Cost & ROI
slides.subtitle: Unified KPI breakdown vs prior year.
slides.footerNote: Conversion rate = Sales ÷ Clicks. AOV = Total Order Value ÷ Sales. ROI = Total Order Value ÷ Total Commission.

slides.title: Program-Level Breakdown: Volume & Conversion
slides.subtitle: Per-program view for selected request scope.

slides.title: KPI Highlights & Business Implications
slides.subtitle: What the numbers mean for the business - key signals and context.
bullets: Conversion Rate Improvement: Conversion rate: data not available. Click volume changed N/A (-) while sales changed N/A (-), indicating a more efficient sales-to-click mix than in the prior year.
Sales Volume Pressure: Total sales changed N/A (-). Click volume changed N/A (-). Largest declining publisher contribution requires confirmation from mover tables.

AOV Growth Partially Offsetting Volume Decline: AOV: data not available. Total order value changed N/A (-) despite lower transaction volume. AOV uplift was concentrated in a smaller set of higher-value publishers.
Rising CPA: CPA: data not available. Publisher commission changed N/A (-) year-over-year, so each conversion carried a higher acquisition cost.
ROI Trend: ROI: data not available. For every unit of commission in the current period, programme return moved from - to -, showing marginal improvement in spend efficiency.
